subject: current-affairs
description: Palestine Israel Issue MCQs for PPSC, FPSC, NTS, CSS & PMS.

mcqs.q: Hamas, the armed Palestinian group, initiated 'Operation Al-Aqsa Flood' on:
mcqs.explanation: Hamas and allied Palestinian groups launched Operation Al-Aqsa Flood against Israel on 7 October 2023.

mcqs.q: Israel launched 'Operation Iron Swords' on:
mcqs.explanation: Israel launched Operation Iron Swords (Iron Sword) on 8 October 2023, the day after the Hamas attack.

mcqs.q: Israel's Security Cabinet formally declared war for the first time since the Yom Kippur War of:
mcqs.explanation: The 8 October 2023 declaration was the first formal war declaration by Israel's Security Cabinet since the Yom Kippur War of 1973.

mcqs.q: Al-Ibrahimi Mosque, which was closed by Israeli troops, is located in the city of:
mcqs.explanation: The Al-Ibrahimi (Ibrahimi) Mosque is located in Al-Khalil (Hebron) in the occupied West Bank.

mcqs.q: Which country has developed a laser-based aerial defence system?
mcqs.explanation: Israel has developed a laser-based aerial defence system named 'Iron Beam'.

mcqs.q: Which two countries called for an urgent UN Security Council meeting after Israeli minister Ben Gvir's visit to Al-Aqsa?
mcqs.explanation: China and the UAE jointly requested an urgent UNSC meeting after Itamar Ben-Gvir's controversial visit to the Al-Aqsa compound.

mcqs.q: What codename has Israel given to its ongoing fight against Hamas?
mcqs.explanation: Israel's military named the operation 'Iron Swords' (sometimes Iron Sword).

mcqs.q: The Gaza Strip is an area between:
options: Israel, Egypt and the Mediterranean Sea
Israel, Egypt and the Red Sea
Israel, Lebanon and the Mediterranean Sea
mcqs.explanation: The Gaza Strip is bordered by Israel, Egypt and the Mediterranean Sea — 41 km long and 10 km wide.

mcqs.q: Roughly how much area of Gaza came under Israeli military control during the war?
mcqs.explanation: By late 2023, Israeli ground forces had operational control over roughly 75% of the Gaza Strip.

mcqs.q: The 7 October 2023 surprise attack on Israel killed _____ Israelis and foreign nationals (including 815 civilians).
mcqs.explanation: Approximately 1,195 Israelis and foreign nationals were killed in the 7 October 2023 attack.

mcqs.q: Since the start of the Israeli offensive in Gaza, over _____ Palestinians have been killed (per available reporting).
mcqs.explanation: Reports indicate over 65,000 Palestinians killed in Gaza since the start of the Israeli offensive.

mcqs.q: Deir el-Balah, recently in the news, is located in:
mcqs.explanation: Deir el-Balah is a city in the central Gaza Strip.

mcqs.q: Nuseirat refugee camp is located in:
mcqs.explanation: Nuseirat is a Palestinian refugee camp in the central Gaza Strip.

mcqs.q: El-Remal area, recently in the news, is located in:
mcqs.explanation: El-Remal (Rimal) is an upscale neighbourhood in Gaza City.

mcqs.q: Who called Gaza 'the city of evil' and urged its residents to leave the city?
mcqs.explanation: Israeli PM Benjamin Netanyahu called Gaza 'the city of evil' early in the war and urged its residents to leave.

mcqs.q: Al-Ahli Arabi Baptist Hospital, recently in the news, is located in:
mcqs.explanation: The Al-Ahli Arabi Baptist Hospital is in Gaza City.

mcqs.q: Jabalia refugee camp, recently in the news, is located in:
mcqs.explanation: Jabalia is the largest refugee camp in the Gaza Strip, in the north.

mcqs.q: A 13-year-old Israeli hostage was released by Hamas during the first ceasefire on:
mcqs.explanation: The 13-year-old Israeli hostage was released by Hamas on 26 November 2023 during the first Qatari-brokered truce.

mcqs.q: Following the introduction of a Qatari-brokered truce on _____, active fighting in the Gaza Strip ceased temporarily.
mcqs.explanation: The first Qatari-brokered truce began on 24 November 2023.

mcqs.q: On 30 November 2023, Hamas released eight hostages in exchange for the release of how many imprisoned Palestinians and a one-day truce extension?
mcqs.explanation: Hamas released 8 hostages in exchange for 30 imprisoned Palestinians and a one-day truce extension on 30 November 2023.

mcqs.q: On which date was Refaat Alareer, a prominent professor and writer in Gaza, killed by an Israeli airstrike?
mcqs.explanation: Refaat Alareer — author of the viral poem 'If I Must Die' — was killed in an Israeli airstrike on 6 December 2023.

mcqs.q: The UN Partition Plan for Palestine — UN Resolution 181 — was passed on:
mcqs.explanation: The UN General Assembly passed Resolution 181 (Partition Plan for Palestine) on 29 November 1947.

mcqs.q: The State of Israel was officially founded on:
mcqs.explanation: David Ben-Gurion declared the establishment of the State of Israel on 14 May 1948.

mcqs.q: The Six-Day War between Israel and Arab states was fought in:
mcqs.explanation: The Six-Day War was fought in June 1967 between Israel and Egypt, Syria and Jordan.

mcqs.q: Hamas, the Palestinian Islamist movement, was founded in:
mcqs.explanation: Hamas was founded in December 1987 by Sheikh Ahmed Yassin during the First Intifada.

mcqs.q: The Palestine Liberation Organization (PLO) was founded in:
mcqs.explanation: The PLO was founded in 1964; Yasser Arafat became its long-time chairman.

mcqs.q: The Oslo Accords between Israel and the PLO were first signed in:
mcqs.explanation: The first Oslo Accord was signed on 13 September 1993 by Yasser Arafat and Yitzhak Rabin in Washington.

mcqs.q: The Abraham Accords — under which the UAE and Bahrain normalised relations with Israel — were signed in:
mcqs.explanation: The Abraham Accords were signed in September 2020, brokered by the first Trump administration.

mcqs.q: The Israeli Prime Minister leading Operation Iron Swords is:
mcqs.explanation: Benjamin Netanyahu (Likud) is the Israeli Prime Minister who leads the war in Gaza.

mcqs.q: Hamas's political leader, killed in Tehran on 31 July 2024, was:
mcqs.explanation: Hamas political bureau chief Ismail Haniyeh was killed in Tehran on 31 July 2024.

mcqs.q: The Hamas Gaza leader killed by Israeli forces in October 2024 was:
mcqs.explanation: Yahya Sinwar — Hamas's Gaza leader and architect of Operation Al-Aqsa Flood — was killed in Rafah on 16 October 2024.

mcqs.q: The long-time Hezbollah Secretary General killed in an Israeli strike in September 2024 was:
mcqs.explanation: Hezbollah Secretary General Hassan Nasrallah was killed in an Israeli airstrike in Beirut on 27 September 2024.

mcqs.q: Israel's main external intelligence agency is:
mcqs.explanation: Mossad is Israel's main external intelligence agency. Shin Bet handles internal security.

mcqs.q: The full name of the Israeli military force is:
mcqs.explanation: Israel's military is officially called the Israel Defense Forces (IDF).

mcqs.q: The Al-Aqsa Mosque is located in:
mcqs.explanation: The Al-Aqsa Mosque is located in the Old City of Jerusalem on the Haram al-Sharif (Temple Mount).

mcqs.q: The Dome of the Rock is located in:
mcqs.explanation: The Dome of the Rock — the gold-domed shrine — sits on the Haram al-Sharif in the Old City of Jerusalem.

mcqs.q: Sheikh Jarrah, a frequently disputed neighbourhood, is located in:
mcqs.explanation: Sheikh Jarrah is a Palestinian neighbourhood in East Jerusalem at the centre of evictions and clashes.

mcqs.q: The international border crossing between Gaza and Egypt is the:
mcqs.explanation: The Rafah crossing connects Gaza to Egypt's Sinai. Erez connects Gaza to Israel for personnel; Kerem Shalom is for goods.

mcqs.q: Which country filed a genocide case against Israel at the ICJ on 29 December 2023?
mcqs.explanation: South Africa filed the genocide case against Israel at the International Court of Justice on 29 December 2023.

mcqs.q: In November 2024, the ICC issued arrest warrants for which Israeli leaders?
mcqs.explanation: On 21 November 2024, the ICC issued arrest warrants for PM Benjamin Netanyahu and former Defence Minister Yoav Gallant.

mcqs.q: The Hamas–Israel ceasefire that took effect on 19 January 2025 was brokered primarily by:
options: Qatar, Egypt and the United States
mcqs.explanation: The 19 January 2025 Gaza ceasefire was brokered primarily by Qatar, Egypt and the United States.
